metadata.from: Bob (Robert) Hanifen — signed 'Bobby,' return address on envelope reads 'B.A. Bob Hanifen 17170600, Sqd. T-BKS-249, Chanute Fld. Ill'
metadata.to: Dad (James Edward Hanifen Sr.) — salutation reads 'Dear Dad'; envelope addressed to Hanifen Body & Paint Co., 904 Keo Way, Des Moines, Iowa
metadata.location_sent_to: Hanifen Body & Paint Co., 904 Keo Way, Des Moines, Iowa
metadata.envelope_notes: Return address: B.A. Bob Hanifen 17170600, Sqd. T-BKS-249, Chanute Fld. Ill. Marked 'free' in upper right corner (military free mail). Envelope is worn and torn along edges.
topics: invitation for dad to visit for Air Forces Day
plans to visit Bloomington canceled due to swollen face
metadata.mood_tone: Light, conversational, slightly complaining about dental pain but generally upbeat; inviting and friendly toward his father.
metadata.legibility_notes: Handwriting is cursive and somewhat hurried; a few words are difficult to parse, particularly 'twinning' or 'twining' (possibly 'sunning'?), and 'better sun tan.' Page 3 is a separate military document (Special Court Martial Orders, 1952) likely stored with this letter but unrelated to the letter itself.
metadata.historical_context: Letter written during WWII in July 1945 (after V-E Day, before V-J Day). Bob is stationed at Chanute Field, IL, a major U.S. Army Air Forces training base. He mentions an Air Forces Day event on August 30. Page 3 is an unrelated 1952 Special Court Martial document concerning a 'Corporal John R. Hanifen' (possibly Bob's brother John) with the 32nd Engineer Construction Group, APO 301, San Francisco — likely Korea-era.
metadata.historical_context_notes: Page 3 (letter41_page3.jpg) is a 1952 Special Court Martial order from Headquarters, 32D Engineer Construction Group, APO 301, c/o Postmaster, San Francisco, California, dated 19 July 1952, reducing Corporal John R. Hanifen (US 55 046 284) to Private E-2 and forfeiting $65.00/month for 3 months. This is almost certainly Bob's brother John Hanifen, serving during the Korean War era. It was apparently stored with this 1945 letter.

pages.transcription:
RETURN ADDRESS (upper left):
B.A. Bob Hanifen 17170600
Sqd. T-BKS-249
Chanute Fld. Ill

POSTMARK: RANTOUL, ILL. / JUL 28 / 11 AM / 1945

UPPER RIGHT: free [military free frank]

ADDRESSED TO:
Hanifen Body & Paint Co.
904 Keo Way
Des Moines, Iowa

Fri.

U. S. ARMY AIR FORCES

Dear Dad

     Well I am thru with the dentist for awhile at last. Went down to see him Wed and he pulled the other tooth, today my jaw is swollen all over my face. Sorry you didn't come in last week. Why don't you drive over for Air Forces day. Aug. 30 it is going to be quite a show. I could ride back with you and have a pretty nice time at home. I was going to go to Bloomia [Bloomington?] this weekend but my face is all out of shape so I think I will stay in camp and take it easy. Was going to the show

today but it was closed when I got there. So I went twinning [sunning?] or I should say wading to get a better sun tan. Well that about all the news in my brain for now.

     Love Bobby

HEADQUARTERS
32D ENGINEER CONSTRUCTION GROUP
APO 301, c/o Postmaster
San Francisco, California

SPECIAL COURT MARTIAL                                    19 July 1952
ORDERS NUMBER     27

     Pursuant to the authority of paragraph 97, MCM, 1951, the unexecuted portion of the sentence to be reduced to the grade of Private E-2 and to forfeit $65.00 per month for 3 months in the case of Corporal John R. Hanifen, US 55 046 284, Company "C" [illegible] 9th Engineer Construction Battalion, APO 301, U.S. Army, as promulgated [in] Special Court Martial Order Number 26, this headquarters, 5 July 1952, is [omitted?].

BY ORDER OF COLONEL CRAMSIE:

OFFICIAL:                             DANIEL L SCHOENLY
                                      1st Lt         CE
[signature: Fred E. Harvey]           Adjutant
FRED E HARVEY
WOJG        USA
Asst Adjutant

DISTRIBUTION:
"A" Plus
  3 – TAG Washington 25, D.C.
  4 – CG EUSAK, KJA
 10 – Individual concerned
 10 – (Add) CO, 439th Engr Const Bn, APO 301

full_transcription:
ENVELOPE:
Return address: B.A. Bob Hanifen 17170600, Sqd. T-BKS-249, Chanute Fld. Ill
Postmark: RANTOUL, ILL. JUL 28, 11 AM, 1945
Addressed to: Hanifen Body & Paint Co., 904 Keo Way, Des Moines, Iowa
Marked: free

---

Fri.

U. S. ARMY AIR FORCES

Dear Dad

     Well I am thru with the dentist for awhile at last. Went down to see him Wed and he pulled the other tooth, today my jaw is swollen all over my face. Sorry you didn't come in last week. Why don't you drive over for Air Forces day. Aug. 30 it is going to be quite a show. I could ride back with you and have a pretty nice time at home. I was going to go to Bloomia [Bloomington?] this weekend but my face is all out of shape so I think I will stay in camp and take it easy. Was going to the show today but it was closed when I got there. So I went twinning [sunning?] or I should say wading to get a better sun tan. Well that about all the news in my brain for now.

     Love Bobby

---

[SEPARATE DOCUMENT — 1952 Court Martial Order]

HEADQUARTERS
32D ENGINEER CONSTRUCTION GROUP
APO 301, c/o Postmaster
San Francisco, California

SPECIAL COURT MARTIAL                                    19 July 1952
ORDERS NUMBER     27

     Pursuant to the authority of paragraph 97, MCM, 1951, the unexecuted portion of the sentence to be reduced to the grade of Private E-2 and to forfeit $65.00 per month for 3 months in the case of Corporal John R. Hanifen, US 55 046 284, Company "C" [illegible] 9th Engineer Construction Battalion, APO 301, U.S. Army, as promulgated [in] Special Court Martial Order Number 26, this headquarters, 5 July 1952, is [omitted?].

BY ORDER OF COLONEL CRAMSIE:

OFFICIAL:                             DANIEL L SCHOENLY
                                      1st Lt         CE
[signature: Fred E. Harvey]           Adjutant
FRED E HARVEY
WOJG        USA
Asst Adjutant

DISTRIBUTION:
"A" Plus
  3 – TAG Washington 25, D.C.
  4 – CG EUSAK, KJA
 10 – Individual concerned
 10 – (Add) CO, 439th Engr Const Bn, APO 301
summary: Written on a Friday in late July 1945, Bob writes to his father from Chanute Field, Illinois, reporting that he has finally finished with the dentist after having another tooth pulled, leaving his jaw swollen. He invites his dad to drive out for Air Forces Day on August 30, noting it will be a great show and Bob could ride home with him afterward. Because of his swollen face, Bob cancels a planned trip to Bloomington and spends the day in camp instead. A separate document stored with the letter is a 1952 Special Court Martial order concerning Corporal John R. Hanifen (almost certainly Bob's brother John), reducing him in grade and forfeiting pay while serving with the 32nd Engineer Construction Group in Korea.
